sanskrit:
महाभूतान्यहङ्कारो बुद्धिरव्यक्तमेव च।
इन्द्रियाणि दशैकं च पञ्च चेन्द्रियगोचराः॥
इच्छा द्वेषः सुखं दुःखं संघातश्चेतना धृतिः।
एतत्क्षेत्रं समासेन सविकारमुदाहृतम्॥
transliteration: mahā-bhūtāny ahaṅkāro buddhir avyaktam eva ca | indriyāṇi daśaikaṃ ca pañca cendriya-gocarāḥ || icchā dveṣaḥ sukhaṃ duḥkhaṃ saṅghātaś cetanā dhṛtiḥ | etat kṣetraṃ samāsena sa-vikāram udāhṛtam ||
literal_meaning: The five great elements, the I-sense, intelligence, the unmanifest, the ten senses plus one (mind), the five sense-objects, desire, aversion, pleasure, pain, the aggregate, consciousness, and steadfastness—this, in brief, is the field together with its modifications.
contextual_meaning: Krishna gives Arjuna a complete inventory of the ‘field’—the embodied psyche—listing every component from gross matter to subtle emotion, showing that all inner and outer experiences are observable contents, not the observing Self.
psychological_state: Meta-cognitive awareness—stepping back to catalogue every sensation, thought, and affect as transient phenomena within the field.
trigger_scenario: During a panic attack you mentally label: ‘rapid heartbeat, fear-thought, urge to flee’—realizing each item is only content appearing in the wider space of awareness.
anchor_text: Witness every thought, feeling, and sensation as passing objects within the field.
